subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Identify the major Mughal emperors from Babur to Aurangzeb
Understand the dual lineage of the Mughals (Genghis Khan and Timur)
Learn about the military campaigns that expanded the empire
Recognize the 'Mansabdari' and 'Zabt' systems of administration
Understand Akbar's policies of 'Sulh-i-kul' and religious tolerance
Identify the roles of important figures like Nur Jahan and Abul Fazl
Learn about the decline of the empire in the late 17th century

lesson_plan.title: Who were the Mughals?
topics_covered: Mughal lineage: Mother's side (Genghis Khan) and Father's side (Timur)
Why they preferred to be called Timurids
Babur and the First Battle of Panipat (1526)
teaching_strategy: Family Tree visualization: Showing the two famous ancestors
Storytelling: Babur's journey from Farghana to Kabul to Delhi
Displaying a portrait of the 'Mughal Family' to show royal pride
student_activities: Draw a simple family tree showing the connection to Timur
Identify the year of the First Battle of Panipat
assessment: Oral quiz: Who was the first Mughal emperor?
Which ancestor did the Mughals prefer to be linked with?

lesson_plan.title: Mughal Military Campaigns
topics_covered: Major campaigns of Humayun and Akbar
Successions: Jahangir, Shah Jahan, and Aurangzeb
Conflict with the Ahoms, Sikhs, and Marathas
teaching_strategy: Map work: Locating the expanding boundaries of the empire
Timeline creation: Listing emperors in chronological order
Briefly explaining the 'War of Succession' among Shah Jahan's sons
student_activities: List the 'Big Six' Mughal emperors in the correct order
Mark Panipat and Agra on a map of India
assessment: Match the emperor to his major opponent (e.g., Akbar vs. Maharana Pratap)
True/False: Aurangzeb had a very long reign

lesson_plan.title: Mansabdars and Jagirdars
topics_covered: Definition of 'Mansab' (Rank/Position)
Zat: Determining rank and salary
Jagirs: Land assignments for revenue collection
teaching_strategy: Analogy: Comparing a Mansabdar to a modern-day officer or manager
Simple Chart: Rank $ightarrow$ Salary $ightarrow$ Number of Horses
Explaining how Mansabdars lived away from their Jagirs
student_activities: Calculate: If a person has a higher 'Zat', will they have more or fewer soldiers?
Write the difference between a Mansabdar and a Jagir
assessment: Short answer: How did Mansabdars get paid?

lesson_plan.title: Zabt and Zamindars
topics_covered: The role of the Zamindar (intermediaries)
Todar Mal’s revenue system (Zabt)
Revenue based on 10-year average crop yields
teaching_strategy: Role-play: Raja Todar Mal explaining the tax system to a farmer
Explaining 'Zabt' as a fair way of collecting taxes based on facts
Discussion: 'Why was revenue collected in cash?'
student_activities: List three crops that were taxed under the Zabt system
Discuss: 'How did Zamindars help the king?'
assessment: What is the meaning of the word 'Zamindar'?

lesson_plan.title: Akbar-nama and Ain-i-Akbari
topics_covered: Abul Fazl and the three-volume history
Contents of Ain-i-Akbari (Administration, Army, Revenues)
Statistical details of the empire
teaching_strategy: Showing a picture of Abul Fazl presenting the book to Akbar
Explaining why written records are important for historians
Discussion on how the book describes the 'diversity' of India
student_activities: Find out what the third volume of the Akbar-nama is called
Write one thing you can find out from the Ain-i-Akbari
assessment: True/False: The Ain-i-Akbari only talks about wars

lesson_plan.title: Akbar’s Policies: Sulh-i-kul
topics_covered: Religious discussions at the Ibadat Khana
The idea of 'Universal Peace' (Sulh-i-kul)
Influence on Jahangir and Shah Jahan
teaching_strategy: Explaining 'Sulh-i-kul' as 'honesty, justice, and peace'
Discussion: 'Why did Akbar talk to people of different religions?'
Visuals of Fatehpur Sikri
student_activities: Create a poster with the words 'Universal Peace'
List three religions Akbar discussed in the Ibadat Khana
assessment: Where did Akbar hold his religious meetings?

lesson_plan.title: The Empire in the 17th Century
topics_covered: Prosperity vs. Poverty: The gap between rich and poor
Decline of authority under the later Mughals
Emergence of new states like Hyderabad and Awadh
teaching_strategy: Comparison: The lifestyle of a noble vs. a peasant
Review of the whole chapter through a 'Fast Facts' quiz
NCERT back-exercise discussion
student_activities: Complete the Mughal Emperor timeline chart
Discuss: 'Why did the empire start becoming weak after Aurangzeb?'
assessment: Oral quiz: Who was the last powerful Mughal ruler?

teaching_aids: NCERT History Textbook: Our Pasts - II
Portrait of the 6 major Mughal Emperors
Map of the Mughal Empire at its peak
teacher_tip: Remind students that 'Mughal' comes from the word 'Mongol'. Even though they didn't like the name, it stuck! Use the comparison of 'Zat' to 'Level Up' in a video game to help them remember rank.
